notes.note: P. Rutilius Lupus, a political associate of Cicero (tribune 56 BC), here acting as courier between Cicero and Decimus Brutus in December 44 BC.
Renders the Latin dateline "a. d. v Idus Dec." --- the fifth day before the Ides of December, counted inclusively, i.e. 9 December (44 BC).

"Regnum," kingship, was the gravest charge in Roman politics; to call a rival's power a bid for monarchy was to brand him an enemy of the free state. Cicero casts Antony's ambitions as a revived tyranny for Decimus to overthrow.
